cards.back: A lexical database for the English language that provides a network of semantic relations between words.
A lexical database that represents words as members of a network of frames, which are conceptual structures that capture the meaning of a word in context.
A program that reduces words to their base or root form, often used to normalize words for stemming or lemmatization.
A tool that identifies the part of speech (such as noun, verb, adjective, etc.) of each word in a sentence or text.
Large collections of text data used to train and evaluate natural language processing models, often used to build dictionaries, thesauri, and other lexical resources.
